accountabilityTheme: AI packets are moving, but the permanent owner-of-record still gets overwritten by last-minute policy and security overrides.
boardQuestion: Which AI lane needs a stable owner ledger before the board trusts the next approval packet?
currentPosture: AI controls are visible, but the owner ledger still changes too often for a board-safe continuity story.
accountabilityHeadline: AI accountability continuity is weaker than the board packet suggests.
accountabilitySignal: Owner-of-record entries are being replaced too often between policy, security, and product.
headline: AI lanes need a durable owner ledger before the next board packet expands scope.
narrative: The board should force the AI owner-of-record model to persist across the full approval chain.
nextMove: Lock one AI owner ledger and require override logging instead of implicit owner changes.

accountabilityTheme: Identity exceptions close slowly because override events are not being written back into one trusted accountability ledger.
boardQuestion: Which identity lane needs a cleaner override record before another exception cycle starts?
currentPosture: Identity accountability exists, but override visibility is still too patchy when audit and security diverge.
accountabilityHeadline: Identity overrides are happening faster than the ledger can explain them.
accountabilitySignal: Final calls are being made, but the owner-of-record history is incomplete after committee overrides.
headline: Identity needs a fuller override ledger before the next audit packet lands.
narrative: The board should require identity overrides to write back into one continuity ledger, not separate committee notes.
nextMove: Record every identity override into one owner continuity ledger before the next exception window.

accountabilityTheme: Revenue packets reopen because the handoff chain is recorded, but the missing ownership gaps are never patched back into one ledger.
boardQuestion: Which reporting lane needs its owner gaps patched before another forecast packet loops back to the board?
currentPosture: The revenue lane is measurable, but accountability continuity remains incomplete across finance and commercial signoffs.
accountabilityHeadline: Revenue ownership gaps are staying open across repeated forecast cycles.
accountabilitySignal: The board packet shows who reviewed the decision, but not who still owns it after the last reopen.
headline: Revenue packets need their open ownership gaps patched before another cycle starts.
narrative: The board should not accept another reopened forecast packet without a patched owner continuity ledger.
nextMove: Patch the owner continuity gaps across finance and commercial signoff before the next forecast review.

accountabilityTheme: FinTech scope looks active, but accountability drift is compounding because too many reviews are changing the owner path without a stable ledger.
boardQuestion: Which capital-sensitive lane needs an accountability-drift review before it can add more scope?
currentPosture: FinTech surfaces are credible, but the accountability story is still too unstable for more expansion.
accountabilityHeadline: FinTech accountability drift is now a scope-quality problem, not just a process problem.
accountabilitySignal: Merchant, treasury, and KYC reviews are all adjusting owner responsibility without a single continuity ledger.
headline: FinTech accountability drift is outrunning the current owner ledger.
narrative: The board should review accountability drift before approving another FinTech scope expansion.
nextMove: Review the FinTech accountability-drift path and freeze owner changes until the ledger is stable.

accountabilityTheme: Procurement remains the cleanest accountability lane and can serve as the reference continuity model.
boardQuestion: Which lane already shows stable owner continuity and can be copied elsewhere?
currentPosture: Procurement closes cleanly because owner continuity survives reviews and overrides are written back into one ledger.
accountabilityHeadline: Procurement already behaves like the accountability reference model.
accountabilitySignal: Trust and commercial packets close with a consistent owner-of-record and low drift.
headline: Procurement shows the accountability-ledger pattern the rest of the estate should copy.
narrative: Use procurement as the reference lane for owner continuity and override discipline.
nextMove: Reuse the procurement continuity ledger in one constrained adjacent lane.

accountabilityTheme: Regulated control lanes still depend on one-off owner resets instead of a durable accountability ledger.
boardQuestion: Which regulated lane needs a stronger owner-of-record ledger before the board scales another control family?
currentPosture: Biotech controls are convincing, but accountability continuity is still too distributed across quality, compliance, and operations.
accountabilityHeadline: Biotech needs stronger accountability-ledger discipline across regulated decisions.
accountabilitySignal: CAPA, specimen, and safety packets still show too many owner shifts between reviews.
headline: Biotech needs a durable accountability ledger before more control-family expansion.
narrative: The board should require regulated owner continuity to persist across the full review chain, not just the reset moment.
nextMove: Lock the biotech owner-of-record ledger and review every regulated owner shift before the next expansion.
